title: Advanced Algorithm Engineering Capstone · Algorithm Design Board
summary: Independently prove, implement, compare, and operate unfamiliar advanced algorithms across graph, optimization, randomized, approximation, and computational-limit scenarios. Planned artifact: Produce a cumulative evidence packet across unfamiliar cases, performance tasks, explanations, and root-cause repairs. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use induction on input size or recursive structure to prove algorithm properties.
Solve recurrences with substitution, recursion trees, or master-method conditions.
Assign credits that cover future expensive operations without negative balances.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-dsa-2 algorithm evidence portfolio."""

from dataclasses import dataclass
from heapq import heappop, heappush
from typing import TypeVar

T = TypeVar('T')

# Add implementation, invariant, counterexample, and measured cost evidence per activity.

steps.title: Plan the cumulative evidence run
steps.instruction: Order the Induction correctness-and-cost proof for this exam task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Induction assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Solve a bounded exam instance with 9 inputs. State the invariant, trace each state change, count the chosen operation, and retain the smallest counterexample. Competency probe: Use induction on input size or recursive structure to prove algorithm properties.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Induction has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
